sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Return visit for IBS with functional abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.
History of Present Illness
Ms. Edwards is a 32-year-old female with a history of irritable bowel syndrome, diagnosed in 2019 and previously treated with bentyl. She reports sensitivity to spicy foods, fatty foods, and alcohol, leading to lower and upper abdominal discomfort. She previously used reglan as needed for nausea. She has experienced diarrhea, particularly after social outings, and reports abdominal cramping and sharp pains. She has been using citrucel regularly with some success. She is currently taking bentyl 20 milligrams as needed, and zofran as needed for nausea. She reports that the symptoms are happening all the time, and she is considering a long-term medication to avoid using so much bentyl or zofran. Elavil was discussed, but the drawback is that it can make her sleepy. Review of symptoms is positive for diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain.
Findings
Physical exam reveals normal lung sounds and a regular heart rhythm. Brittany Edwards is a pleasant female with IBS and functional abdominal pain, aggravated by high stress, poor eating habits, and poor sleeping habits.
Assessment
The patient presents with irritable bowel syndrome.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: The patient was instructed to start a low dose fiber supplement, such as citrucel, daily, and probiotics to help with gas and bloating. She should take bentyl up to four times daily for intestinal cramping. She should stop reglan and use zofran as needed. Neuromodulators such as elavil and buspar were discussed but not started. Follow-up will be arranged through mychart email in approximately one month to assess symptom management. Dietary and lifestyle modifications are recommended, and elavil 25 milligrams at bedtime for IBS is prescribed.
